content.text:
Memory stage: EntityCanonicalizer v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Stage instructions:
        You are EntityCanonicalizer v1.

Goal:
Resolve entity mentions from the latest turns into canonical entities.

Return JSON object:
{
  "operations": [
    {
      "mention": "text",
      "action": "link_existing | create_new | add_alias | noop",
      "entity_id": "uuid-or-null",
      "new_entity": {
        "entity_type": "user | person | agent | organization | project | repo | file | technology | product | location | concept | document | conversation | service | environment | other",
        "canonical_name": "text",
        "summary": "text-or-null"
      },
      "about_file_assertion": false,
      "alias_text": "text-or-null",
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "reason": "short explanation"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Prefer precision over recall.
- Do not merge distinct entities on weak evidence.
- Create new entities only when no candidate is a safe match.
- If a candidate safely matches the mention, use "link_existing" with that candidate id.
- Use "add_alias" only when the mention is a new surface form for a safe existing candidate.
- Canonical names are identity keys inside a namespace: the same canonical_name is treated as the same entity even if the entity_type differs.
- If distinct referents share the same surface form, disambiguate the canonical_name, for example "Java (programming language)" vs "Java (island)".
- If several candidates share a name, pick by entity type and summary; do not create another same-name entity just because the type differs.
- Prefer English for entity summaries; keep proper names, product names, repo names, and file names unchanged.
- Entity summaries must describe identity only, not mutable facts, current status, preferences, ownership, formats, fields, versions, or decisions. Put those facts into claims/notes instead.
- For USER first-person facts and preferences, resolve the user as the stable namespace-level USER entity named "User".
- For imported document/file_path/raw_url sources, the source path, URL, title, and section heading are source metadata. Do not create DOCUMENT, FILE, or URL-like entities merely to represent the imported source itself. Create a document/file entity only when the document content discusses that document/file as a durable domain object.
- For imported document sources, do not create FILE entities from incidental code references, stack traces, code blocks, file extensions, or paths such as "*.kt". Set action="noop" unless the text states a durable assertion about the file itself and later stages need that file as a claim subject or object.
- Set about_file_assertion=true only for FILE operations where the target text asserts something about that file itself. Set it false for non-FILE operations and incidental file-like mentions.
- Do not use raw labels such as "user", "assistant", "project", or "document" as entity_id. entity_id must be an existing candidate id, or null when creating a new entity.
- Use relevant retrieval context to resolve ambiguous target mentions such as pronouns, "it", "that", "first one", "second one", and "from that list".
- When TARGET_MESSAGE selects one item from a previously retrieved ordered list, create or link only the selected concrete entity so later stages can write the target claim.
- Never create chat-specific USER entities such as "User of chat:..."; a new chat is not a new user.
- Return valid JSON only.

        Detected raw mentions:
        No deterministic mention detector is available in this MVP.
Infer only concrete reusable referents from the latest turns.
For first-person USER statements, create or link a stable USER entity when claims need a subject.

        Candidate existing entities:
        - id=entity:<0>; type=CONCEPT; name=Scope and lineage (memory architecture axis); aliases=none; summary=Concept named Scope and lineage (memory architecture axis).
- id=entity:<1>; type=CONCEPT; name=Fast path (memory architecture); aliases=none; summary=Concept named Fast path (memory architecture).
- id=entity:<2>; type=CONCEPT; name=Read path (memory architecture); aliases=none; summary=Concept named Read path (memory architecture).
- id=entity:<3>; type=CONCEPT; name=Slow path (memory architecture); aliases=none; summary=Concept named Slow path (memory architecture).
- id=entity:<4>; type=CONCEPT; name=Write path (memory architecture); aliases=none; summary=Concept named Write path (memory architecture).
- id=entity:<5>; type=CONCEPT; name=Lifecycle (memory architecture axis); aliases=none; summary=Concept named Lifecycle (memory architecture axis).
- id=entity:<6>; type=CONCEPT; name=Memory v3 architecture; aliases=none; summary=Concept named Memory v3 architecture.
- id=entity:<7>; type=CONCEPT; name=Representation (memory architecture axis); aliases=none; summary=Concept named Representation (memory architecture axis).

        Relevant retrieval context for ambiguous mention resolution:
        - source external:document-section:9ab04f0af9d4842289e56dca233345ed: Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB
Document lines: 547-558

## 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB

`Entity + Claim + NoteLink + scope_json` уже дают graph-like semantics.
Для memory layer обычно хватает:

- Postgres
- JSONB
- pgvector
- full-text indexes
- lineage tables

Graph DB нужен, когда traversal/path queries становятся частью продукта, а не просто внутренним удобством.
- source chat:<0> Memory ingestion source document. Treat the following as a pasted handoff architecture document, not as an immediate task.

File: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md

# 01. Architecture

## 1. Цель системы

Нужна memory layer для AI-агента, которая:

- переживает границы одной сессии
- умеет хранить факты, preferences, задачи, rationale и evolving plans
- поддерживает обновление знаний, а не только накопление
- позволяет объяснить, **откуда** взялась память
- не превращает каждый turn в permanent knowledge
- умеет старить, архивировать и чинить память

## 2. Центральная архитектурная идея

В `v3` у памяти четыре оси, а не одна:

1. **Representation**
   - `Source`
   - `Claim`
   - `Note
[truncated 10644 chars]
- source external:document-section:f5dcd6c9392e5433046cb73719dbe2d9: Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 5. Storage tiers .. 6. Structured scope
Document lines: 127-208

## 5. Storage tiers

### Tier A. Core / in-context memory
Это маленькие блоки, которые почти всегда идут в prompt.

Что туда класть:
- краткий user profile
- active tasks
- очень короткий session summary
- system-level behavior preferences

Что туда не класть:
- все claims подряд
- все notes подряд
- raw transcript
- длинные списки фактов

### Tier B. Structured long-term memory
Основное typed memory-хранилище.

Типы:
- `Entity`
- `Claim`
- `Profile`
- `Task`
- `Episode`
- `Source`
- `MemoryRun`

### Ti
[truncated 1131 chars]
- note hot-path:note:<0> Storage tiers in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md; The storage model defines five tiers: Tier A Core / in-context memory for small prompt-ready blocks, Tier B Structured long-term memory for typed objects, Tier C Rich note memory for semantic context, Tier D Raw evidence for immutable source material, and Tier E Retrieval indexes for vector, full-text, and filtered retrieval.
- claim hot-path:claim:<0> The Retrieval indexes storage tier indexes memory content for retrieval using vector indexes, full-text indexes, and filters.
- note hot-path:note:<1> Recommended memory architecture flow; The recommended architecture logs incoming User / Tool / Document sources in Source Log, routes them through Memory Router and Write-Time Retrieval, canonicalizes entities, then sends work to Claim Extractor, Task Updater, Profile Updater, and Note Constructor before reconciliation into Postgres + pgvector. Stored memory is maintained by Background Consolidator, Memory Repair Worker, and Retention / Forgetting Worker, and read-time answering flows through Assistant Runtime, Read-Time Retrieval Planner, Postgres + pgvector, and Answer Composer.
- note hot-path:note:<2> Responsibilities split between User-facing runtime, Memory worker / manager, and Storage layer; In agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, responsibility is split so the User-facing runtime handles dialogue, tool orchestration, read-time retrieval, and cheap routing/escalation; it must not carry heavy reconciliation and repair. The Memory worker / manager owns routing, retrieval-before-update, canonicalization, extraction, reconciliation, consolidation, repair, and retention, while the Storage layer owns immutable evidence, typed tables, transactional updates, lineage, indexes, and audit trail.
- claim hot-path:claim:<1> The Rich note memory storage tier serves as an intermediate semantic layer for design direction, rationale, local conclusions, plan fragments, document digests, and lessons.
- note hot-path:note:<3> Memory write model separates semantic and maintenance paths; In agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, the write model distinguishes two semantic write paths and two maintenance paths: Direct structured path for safely normalized facts, preferences, commitments/tasks, and status changes; Note path for important but not-yet-hard-fact material such as rationale, trade-offs, evolving plans, lessons, document digests, and contextual framing. Consolidation promotes mature notes into structured memory when signal is strong enough, while Repair fixes memory degradation such as duplicates, stale or contradictory items, note spam, profile drift, and bad merges.
- note hot-path:note:<4> Write path operational sequence; In agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, the Write path persists raw input, routes it, retrieves candidate memories before extraction, canonicalizes entities, extracts claims/notes/tasks/profile updates, reconciles candidates with existing items, applies all memory operations in one transaction, and schedules maintenance such as consolidation, duplicate merge, stale cleanup, profile repair, alias merge, and retention/archive.
- claim hot-path:claim:<2> The memory system has Note path as a semantic write path.
- note hot-path:note:<5> Read path retrieval modes, budgets, and answer composition rules; In agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, the Read path first decides whether memory is needed for user-specific, history-aware, task-aware, rationale-heavy, temporal, or high-stakes questions, then loads core blocks, chooses Claim-first, Note-first, Mixed, or Evidence-first / evidence-fallback retrieval, respects explicit v3 budget caps, and composes answers by distinguishing verified vs provisional information, handling conflicts, respecting scope, abstaining when needed, and not presenting notes as hard facts without qualification.

Для memory layer обычно хватает: - Postgres - JSONB - pgvector - full-text indexes - lineage tables Graph DB нужен, когда traversal/path queries становятся частью продукта, а не просто внутренним удобством.
